title: Would ban cloud storage, AI tracking, and federal data access
description: H4675 is the strongest of the four bills. It bans third-party cloud storage of camera data, prohibits AI-based vehicle tracking beyond license plates, sets a 21-day retention limit, requires warrants for data access, and explicitly blocks federal data access. If passed, every existing Flock Safety contract in SC would be void.

title: Would regulate license plate reader systems statewide
description: S447 cleared a Senate Judiciary subcommittee in April 2025, but the full committee hasn't acted. This bill would establish statewide rules for how law enforcement can use license plate readers, including 90-day data retention limits and access controls.

title: Would regulate automatic license plate readers
description: H3155 sits in the House Judiciary Committee with no action taken. It would regulate how automatic license plate readers are deployed and require transparency about data sharing with other agencies, including federal law enforcement.

title: Would regulate automated license plate readers
status: In Committee, House Education and Public Works
lastAction: Member(s) request name added as sponsor: Alexander
description: H4013 is in the House Education and Public Works Committee with no action taken. It addresses automated license plate reader regulation, focusing on deployment oversight and data access policies.
